extraction.one_line_summary: SOLER Marc soloed to victory after attacking on the descent from Alto San Miguel, gaining 19 seconds on ROGLIČ who sprinted for bonus seconds to protect the overall lead.
tactical_patterns: WELLENS Tim attacked 40 miles out on category-two climb using diesel power rather than waiting for steep Alto San Miguel finale.
Movistar pre-positioned VERONA Carlos to pace early Alto San Miguel kilometers before SOLER attacked during descent hesitation moment, revealing rehearsed sequencing.
ROGLIČ Primož prioritized sprint bonus seconds over chasing SOLER, calculating 6-second bonus offset against 19-second stage loss to protect overall by mathematics.
Crosswind tactics in Navarra region proved crucial for Movistar despite GC already split on Stage 1, showing importance beyond opening stage.

rider_signals.SOLER Marc: positive: Won from descent attack after teammate pacing setup, moved 20th to 10th overall, showing Movistar rehearsed local road knowledge advantage.
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: Tactically chose sprint bonus over stage chase, extending overall lead through calculated risk management rather than reactive racing.
rider_signals.WELLENS Tim: neutral: Solo attack 40 miles out on shallow category-two suggests misjudged effort distribution versus saving energy for steeper Alto San Miguel.
rider_signals.GROßSCHARTNER Felix: negative: Lost significant time after strong Stage 1 fifth place, indicating vulnerability on extended steep climbs versus punchy finishes.
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: positive: Stayed positioned on ROGLIČ wheel in finale rather than own effort, third place finish via smart parasitic tactics.
rider_signals.VERONA Carlos: positive: Executed specific pacing role on early Alto San Miguel kilometers before SOLER takeover, revealing Movistar's planned team sequence.
rider_signals.MAS Enric: neutral: Retained best young rider jersey with 1:29 over BAGIOLI despite being dropped earlier, showing recovery capacity but initial weakness.
